# DES_METADATA
Step: 01-02
Feature: skills
Command: /nw-execute

# AGENT_IDENTITY
Agent: nw-functional-software-crafter

# SKILL_LOADING
Before starting TDD phases, read your skill files for methodology guidance.
Skills path: ~/.claude/skills/nw-functional-software-crafter/SKILL.md
Always load at PREPARE: tdd-methodology.md, quality-framework.md
Load on-demand per phase as specified in your Skill Loading Strategy table.

# TASK_CONTEXT
Step: 01-02
Name: Skill domain types
Description: Create app/src/server/skill/types.ts with SkillStatus, SkillSourceType, SkillSource, SkillRecord, CreateSkillInput, SkillListItem, SkillDetail types. Follow learning/types.ts pattern.

Acceptance Criteria:
- SkillStatus = "draft" | "active" | "deprecated"
- SkillSourceType = "github" | "git"
- SkillSource has type, source, optional ref/subpath/skills
- CreateSkillInput has name, description, version, source, optional required_tool_ids
- SkillListItem has id, name, description, version, status, source, required_tools, agent_count, created_at
- SkillDetailResponse has skill, required_tools, agents, governed_by
- No null types -- use optional fields only (field?: Type)

Test File: tests/acceptance/skill/walking-skeleton.test.ts
Scenario Name: WS-1 (types are prerequisite, no standalone test)

Files to Modify:
- app/src/server/skill/types.ts (NEW)

Implementation Notes:
- Read app/src/server/learning/ directory for the pattern (if it exists, read the types file)
- Read app/src/server/agents/types.ts for existing patterns
- Use RecordId<"skill", string> for SkillRecord
- SkillSource fields: type (SkillSourceType), source (string), ref? (string), subpath? (string), skills? (string[])
- SkillListItem.required_tools: Array<{ id: string; name: string }>
- SkillDetailResponse.governed_by: Array<{ id: string; name: string; status: string }>
- SkillDetailResponse.agents: Array<{ id: string; name: string }>
- Use readonly on SkillSource fields per architecture spec

This is a types-only step. After creating the types, proceed immediately to creating the query functions (step 01-03) in the same session.

AFTER completing 01-02, also execute step 01-03:

Step: 01-03
Name: Skill query functions: create and list
Description: Create app/src/server/skill/skill-queries.ts with createSkill (INSERT + skill_requires edges, app-layer name uniqueness check) and listSkills (with optional status filter). Follow learning system pattern with surreal as first parameter.

Acceptance Criteria:
- createSkill takes surreal, workspaceId, input, identityId as parameters
- Pre-validates name uniqueness via SELECT query before CREATE
- Creates skill record with status "draft" and workspace reference
- Creates skill_requires edges for each required_tool_id via RELATE
- Returns created skill with id and status
- listSkills returns skills filtered by workspace and optional status
- Each list item includes required_tools and agent_count via graph traversal

Test File: tests/acceptance/skill/skill-crud.test.ts
Scenario Name: C-1

Files to Modify:
- app/src/server/skill/skill-queries.ts (NEW)

Implementation Notes:
- Read existing query patterns: app/src/server/agents/agent-queries.ts and any learning query files
- Read docs/agents/surrealdb.md for query conventions (RecordId, batching, protected variables, etc.)
- surreal as first parameter (dependency injection)
- Use RecordId objects for all SurrealDB identifiers
- Name uniqueness: SELECT id FROM skill WHERE workspace = $ws AND name = $name LIMIT 1
- Create: CREATE $skill CONTENT {...} — use RecordId for the skill record
- skill_requires edges: RELATE $skill->skill_requires->$tool for each tool
- Batch queries in single .query() call where possible
- listSkills query needs to join skill_requires and possesses for required_tools and agent_count
- For agent_count: SELECT count() FROM possesses WHERE out = $skill
- For required_tools: SELECT out.id, out.name FROM skill_requires WHERE in = $skill
- Consider using a single batch query for list items
- HttpError(409) for duplicate name, HttpError(404) for not found
- Import HttpError from the existing http/errors.ts module

# DESIGN_CONTEXT
Architecture: Modular monolith, functional TypeScript (Bun backend + React frontend). SurrealDB graph database.
Module: New `skill/` at `app/src/server/skill/` (skill-route.ts, skill-queries.ts, types.ts).
Pattern: Learning system — route handler factory with DI, query functions taking `surreal` as first param.
Key conventions:
- No classes, functional style
- RecordId objects everywhere for SurrealDB identifiers
- No null — optional fields use `field?: Type`
- Query batching: combine related queries in single .query() call
- HttpError for error responses (400, 404, 409)
- Application-layer name uniqueness (not DB UNIQUE index)

# QUALITY_GATES
- All types match design spec from architecture.md Section 6.2
- Query functions follow existing patterns (learning/agent queries)
- No null types anywhere
- TypeScript compiles without errors

# TDD_PHASES
Execute in order:
0. PREPARE - Read existing patterns (learning system, agent queries, surrealdb.md conventions)
1. RED_ACCEPTANCE - For 01-02 (types only): skip (types have no standalone test). For 01-03: the C-1 test in skill-crud.test.ts exists but is skipped — read it to understand what it expects.

+DEFINE TABLE skill SCHEMAFULL;
+DEFINE FIELD name ON skill TYPE string;
+DEFINE FIELD description ON skill TYPE string;
+DEFINE FIELD version ON skill TYPE string;
+DEFINE FIELD status ON skill TYPE string ASSERT $value IN ["draft", "active", "deprecated"];

+DEFINE FIELD source.source ON skill TYPE string;
+DEFINE FIELD source.ref ON skill TYPE option<string>;
+DEFINE FIELD source.subpath ON skill TYPE option<string>;
+DEFINE FIELD source.skills ON skill TYPE option<array<string>>;
+DEFINE FIELD created_by ON skill TYPE option<record<identity>>;
+DEFINE FIELD created_at ON skill TYPE datetime;
+DEFINE FIELD updated_at ON skill TYPE option<datetime>;
+DEFINE INDEX idx_skill_workspace_name ON skill FIELDS workspace, name;
+-- skill_requires: skill requires mcp_tool
+DEFINE TABLE skill_requires TYPE RELATION IN skill OUT mcp_tool SCHEMAFULL;
+-- possesses: identity possesses skill
+DEFINE TABLE possesses TYPE RELATION IN identity OUT skill SCHEMAFULL;
+DEFINE FIELD granted_at ON possesses TYPE datetime;
+-- skill_supersedes: skill version chain
+DEFINE TABLE skill_supersedes TYPE RELATION IN skill OUT skill SCHEMAFULL;
+-- skill_evidence: skill activation evidence
+DEFINE TABLE skill_evidence TYPE RELATION IN skill OUT agent_session | trace | observation SCHEMAFULL;
+DEFINE FIELD added_at ON skill_evidence TYPE datetime;
+-- governs_skill: policy governance edge from policy to skill
+DEFINE TABLE governs_skill TYPE RELATION IN policy OUT skill SCHEMAFULL;

input.prompt:
<!-- DES-VALIDATION : required -->
<!-- DES-PROJECT-ID : skills -->
<!-- DES-STEP-ID : 02-01 -->

# DES_METADATA
Step: 02-01
Feature: skills
Command: /nw-execute

# AGENT_IDENTITY
Agent: nw-functional-software-crafter

# SKILL_LOADING
Before starting TDD phases, read your skill files for methodology guidance.
Skills path: ~/.claude/skills/nw-functional-software-crafter/SKILL.md
Always load at PREPARE: tdd-methodology.md, quality-framework.md

# TASK_CONTEXT
This session covers FOUR steps sequentially: 02-01, 02-02, 02-03, and 02-04.

## Step 02-01: Skill detail query and route handler
Description: Add getSkillDetail to skill-queries.ts: returns skill with required_tools (via skill_requires), agents (via possesses), governed_by (via governs_skill). Add handleGetDetail route handler and register the GET route.

Acceptance Criteria:
- getSkillDetail returns skill metadata + required_tools array + agents array + governed_by array
- required_tools resolved via: SELECT out.id, out.name FROM skill_requires WHERE in = $skill
- agents resolved via: SELECT in.name FROM possesses WHERE out = $skill (get identity name, then agent name)
- governed_by resolved via: SELECT in.id, in.name, in.status FROM governs_skill WHERE out = $skill
- GET /api/workspaces/:wsId/skills/:skillId returns 200 with SkillDetailResponse
- Returns 404 for nonexistent skill

Files to Modify: app/src/server/skill/skill-queries.ts, app/src/server/skill/skill-route.ts, app/src/server/runtime/start-server.ts

## Step 02-02: Skill update query and route handler
Description: Add updateSkill to skill-queries.ts and handleUpdate route handler. Supports updating name, description, version, source, required_tool_ids. Sets updated_at.

Acceptance Criteria:
- updateSkill updates provided fields and sets updated_at
- If name changes, validates new name uniqueness within workspace
- If required_tool_ids provided, replaces skill_requires edges (delete old, create new)
- PUT /api/workspaces/:wsId/skills/:skillId returns 200 with updated skill
- Returns 404 for nonexistent skill

Files to Modify: app/src/server/skill/skill-queries.ts, app/src/server/skill/skill-route.ts, app/src/server/runtime/start-server.ts

## Step 02-03: Skill delete query and route handler
Description: Add deleteSkill to skill-queries.ts: validates 0 possesses edges, removes skill + skill_requires edges. Add handleDelete route handler.

Acceptance Criteria:
- deleteSkill checks possesses edge count, throws HttpError(409) if > 0
- Deletes skill record and all skill_requires edges in transaction
- DELETE /api/workspaces/:wsId/skills/:skillId returns 200 on success
- Returns 409 when agents possess the skill (C-9)
- Returns 404 for nonexistent skill

Files to Modify: app/src/server/skill/skill-queries.ts, app/src/server/skill/skill-route.ts, app/src/server/runtime/start-server.ts

## Step 02-04: Enable remaining CRUD and lifecycle test scenarios
Description: Enable ALL skipped tests in skill-crud.test.ts and skill-lifecycle.test.ts. Fix any issues found.

Acceptance Criteria:
- C-1 through C-11 all pass
- L-1 through L-5 all pass
- All tests in tests/acceptance/skill/skill-crud.test.ts pass
- All tests in tests/acceptance/skill/skill-lifecycle.test.ts pass

Files to Modify: tests/acceptance/skill/skill-crud.test.ts, tests/acceptance/skill/skill-lifecycle.test.ts

## Implementation Notes
- Read the existing code: app/src/server/skill/skill-queries.ts, skill-route.ts, types.ts
- Read app/src/server/runtime/start-server.ts for route registration pattern (see how /api/workspaces/:workspaceId/skills routes are registered for create/list/activate/deprecate)
- For detail route: register GET at /api/workspaces/:workspaceId/skills/:skillId — careful about route ordering (this is a catch-all for skillId, put it after more specific routes like activate/deprecate)
- For update: PUT at /api/workspaces/:workspaceId/skills/:skillId
- For delete: DELETE at /api/workspaces/:workspaceId/skills/:skillId
- Read tests/acceptance/skill/skill-crud.test.ts to understand what each C-* scenario expects
- Read tests/acceptance/skill/skill-lifecycle.test.ts for L-* scenarios
- Read tests/acceptance/skill/skill-test-kit.ts for available helpers
- For name check route: if there's a check-name endpoint referenced in tests, add it too
- Use RecordId objects for all SurrealDB queries
- Batch queries in single .query() call where possible
- HttpError(404) for not found, HttpError(409) for conflicts
- When enabling tests, change `it.skip(` to `it(` — DO NOT change test logic

After enabling tests, run them:
bun test tests/acceptance/skill/skill-crud.test.ts
bun test tests/acceptance/skill/skill-lifecycle.test.ts

ALL tests must pass. Fix any issues before committing.

# DESIGN_CONTEXT
Architecture: Modular monolith, functional TypeScript (Bun backend + React frontend). SurrealDB graph database.
Module: `app/src/server/skill/` (skill-route.ts, skill-queries.ts, types.ts).
Pattern: Handler factory function with DI. Query functions take surreal as first param.
Route registration in start-server.ts uses withTracing() wrapper.
API endpoints needed:
- GET /api/workspaces/:wsId/skills/:skillId → handleGetDetail (200)
- PUT /api/workspaces/:wsId/skills/:skillId → handleUpdate (200)
- DELETE /api/workspaces/:wsId/skills/:skillId → handleDelete (200)

Existing routes already registered:
- POST/GET /api/workspaces/:wsId/skills
- POST /api/workspaces/:wsId/skills/:skillId/activate
- POST /api/workspaces/:wsId/skills/:skillId/deprecate

# QUALITY_GATES
- All C-1 through C-11 tests pass
- All L-1 through L-5 tests pass
- bun run typecheck passes
- No regressions in WS-1

# TDD_PHASES
Execute in order:
0. PREPARE - Read existing code and test files
1. RED_ACCEPTANCE - Read the skipped tests to understand expectations. Run tests to confirm they fail.
2. RED_UNIT - Skip (acceptance tests provide coverage)
3. GREEN - Implement detail, update, delete. Register routes. Enable tests. Fix until all pass.
4. COMMIT - Four commits (one per step: 02-01, 02-02, 02-03, 02-04).
   Use --no-verify and -s flags for git commit.
   Include Step-ID trailer in each commit.

Record DES phases for ALL FOUR steps separately.

# OUTCOME_RECORDING
After ACTUALLY EXECUTING each phase, record via DES CLI:

    PYTHONPATH=$HOME/.claude/lib/python python3 -m des.cli.log_phase \
      --project-dir docs/feature/skills/deliver \
      --step-id {02-01|02-02|02-03|02-04} \

## Step 05-01: Skill hooks and API client
Description: Create useSkills, useSkillDetail, useCreateSkill, useActivateSkill, useDeprecateSkill hooks in app/src/client/hooks/use-skills.ts. Follow existing hook patterns.

Acceptance Criteria:
- useSkills fetches GET /api/workspaces/:wsId/skills with optional status filter
- useSkillDetail fetches GET /api/workspaces/:wsId/skills/:skillId
- useCreateSkill POSTs to /api/workspaces/:wsId/skills
- useActivateSkill POSTs to /api/workspaces/:wsId/skills/:skillId/activate
- useDeprecateSkill POSTs to /api/workspaces/:wsId/skills/:skillId/deprecate
- Hooks handle loading, error, and success states

Files to Modify: app/src/client/hooks/use-skills.ts (NEW)

## Step 05-02: Skill Library list page
Description: Create SkillLibraryPage with card-based list, status filter tabs, empty state. Register route in TanStack Router. Add navigation link.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Route: /workspaces/:id/skills (inside the workspace layout)
- Cards show name, version, status badge, description excerpt, source type, agent count
- Status filter tabs: All, Active, Draft, Deprecated
- Empty state with guidance text and Create Skill button
- Navigation link added to sidebar/nav

Files to Modify: app/src/client/routes/skill-library-page.tsx (NEW), app/src/client/components/skill/skill-card.tsx (NEW)

## Step 05-03: Skill detail page
Description: Create SkillDetailPage showing full metadata, required tools, assigned agents, governance info, and lifecycle action buttons.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Route: /workspaces/:id/skills/:skillId
- Shows skill name, description, version, status, source info
- Required tools section lists tool names
- Agents section lists agent names
- Governed By section lists policy names
- Activate button shown for draft skills
- Deprecate button shown for active skills

Files to Modify: app/src/client/routes/skill-detail-page.tsx (NEW)

## Step 05-04: Create Skill form page
Description: Create SkillCreatePage with form: name, description, version, source type radio, repository/URL, ref, subpath, required tools checklist. Inline validation.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Route: /workspaces/:id/skills/new
- Source type radio toggles field labels (Repository vs URL)
- Required tools fetched from workspace mcp_tool catalog (if endpoint available, otherwise skip tool selection for MVP)
- Inline validation for required fields
- Submit creates skill and redirects to Skill Library
- Created skill defaults to draft status

Files to Modify: app/src/client/routes/skill-create-page.tsx (NEW)

## Implementation Notes
READ THESE FILES FIRST to understand existing patterns:
- app/src/client/hooks/use-agents.ts and use-agent-actions.ts — existing hook patterns
- app/src/client/routes/agent-create-page.tsx — existing page pattern

Route registration:

- Add parallel routes for /skills, /skills/new, /skills/$skillId

<!-- DES-VALIDATION : required -->
<!-- DES-PROJECT-ID : skills -->
<!-- DES-STEP-ID : 06-01 -->

# DES_METADATA
Step: 06-01
Feature: skills
Command: /nw-execute

# AGENT_IDENTITY
Agent: nw-functional-software-crafter

# SKILL_LOADING
Before starting TDD phases, read your skill files for methodology guidance.
Skills path: ~/.claude/skills/nw-functional-software-crafter/SKILL.md
Always load at PREPARE: tdd-methodology.md, quality-framework.md

# TASK_CONTEXT
This session covers TWO steps sequentially: 06-01 and 06-02.

## Step 06-01: Wizard Step 1 — consolidated config (runtime radio + form)
Description: Rewrite AgentCreatePage as 3-step wizard with useState state management. Step 1 consolidates runtime radio + agent form fields (name, description, model, scopes, sandbox config).

Acceptance Criteria:
- Runtime is a radio group (Sandbox/External), not separate step
- Sandbox config fields shown conditionally when Sandbox selected
- Name uniqueness validated via existing check-name API
- Next button enabled only when required fields valid
- Wizard state preserved across steps (useState at page level)
- Step indicators at top (Step 1 of 3, Step 2 of 3, Step 3 of 3)
- Back button on steps 2 and 3

Files to Modify: app/src/client/routes/agent-create-page.tsx (REWRITE), app/src/client/components/agent/wizard-step-config.tsx (NEW)

## Step 06-02: Wizard Step 2 (Skills) + Step 3 (Tools)
Description: Step 2 shows active workspace skills as checklist with count badge. Step 3 shows skill-derived tools (read-only) and additional tools (editable). Create Agent button on Step 3 submits with skill_ids and additional_tool_ids.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Step 2 fetches active skills and shows checklist with name, version, description
- Count badge updates on check/uncheck
- Skip button advances with 0 skills
- Empty state when no active skills
- Step 3 shows skill-derived tools with "via skill X" labels (client-side resolution from skill's required_tools)
- Shared tools listed once with all source skill names
- Additional tools section (editable checklist) — for MVP, this can show a placeholder if no tool listing API exists
- Total effective tools summary shown
- Create Agent submits with skill_ids and additional_tool_ids to POST /api/workspaces/:wsId/agents
- Back navigation preserves selections
- External agents: skills step shows info that skills are for sandbox agents only

Files to Modify: app/src/client/components/agent/wizard-step-skills.tsx (NEW), app/src/client/components/agent/wizard-step-tools.tsx (NEW)

## Implementation Notes
READ THESE FILES FIRST:
- app/src/client/routes/agent-create-page.tsx — CURRENT 2-step wizard (you will REWRITE this)
- app/src/client/components/agent/authority-scope-form.tsx — reuse as-is
- app/src/client/components/agent/proxy-token-dialog.tsx — reuse as-is
- app/src/client/hooks/use-agent-actions.ts — existing createAgent hook
- app/src/client/hooks/use-skills.ts — skill hooks you created in 05-01
- app/src/client/hooks/use-agents.ts — existing hooks pattern
- app/src/client/components/layout/WorkspaceSidebar.tsx — for workspace state pattern

Key design decisions (from DISCOVER wave):
- D1: 3-step wizard (Config > Skills > Tools)
- D2: Minimal checklist for skill assignment in Step 2
- D3: Two-section tool display in Step 3 (skill-derived read-only + manual selection)
- D4: Steps 2 and 3 are skippable
- D5: Runtime selection consolidated into Step 1 as radio group
- D18: React useState at AgentCreatePage level with props drilling to step components

Wizard state structure:
```
AgentCreatePage (state owner)
  |-- step: 1 | 2 | 3
  |-- configState: { runtime, name, description, model, scopes, sandboxConfig }
  |-- selectedSkillIds: string[]
  |-- additionalToolIds: string[]
  |
  +-- WizardStepConfig (step 1) — runtime radio + all form fields
  +-- WizardStepSkills (step 2) — active skills checklist
  +-- WizardStepTools (step 3) — skill-derived + additional tools + Create button
```

For the tools step (Step 3):
- Skill-derived tools are computed client-side from the skills fetched in Step 2
- Each skill in the list API response includes `required_tools: Array<{ id, name }>`
- Deduplicate tools, show "via skill X" labels
- Additional tools: if there's a workspace tools/mcp_tool list API, use it. If not, show a placeholder or skip this section for MVP.

Submit on Step 3 calls the existing createAgent hook with the additional fields:
- skill_ids: selectedSkillIds
- additional_tool_ids: additionalToolIds

After successful creation:
- External agents: show ProxyTokenDialog (existing behavior)
- Sandbox agents: navigate to /agents

# DESIGN_CONTEXT
Architecture: React frontend with TanStack Router, Zustand workspace store.
Rewriting agent-create-page.tsx from 2-step to 3-step wizard.
Step components receive state + callbacks as props.
No external state library — React useState at page level.

# QUALITY_GATES
- bun run typecheck passes
- Existing agent creation still works (external and sandbox flows)
- Step navigation works correctly
- No regressions in other routes

# TDD_PHASES
Execute in order:
0. PREPARE - Read existing wizard code and all related components
1. RED_ACCEPTANCE - Skip (no frontend acceptance tests)
2. RED_UNIT - Skip (no frontend unit tests for MVP)
3. GREEN - Rewrite wizard, create step components, verify typecheck passes
4. COMMIT - Two commits (06-01 and 06-02). Use --no-verify and -s. Include Step-ID trailers.

Record DES phases for BOTH steps.

# OUTCOME_RECORDING
After ACTUALLY EXECUTING each phase, record via DES CLI:

    PYTHONPATH=$HOME/.claude/lib/python python3 -m des.cli.log_phase \
      --project-dir docs/feature/skills/deliver \
      --step-id {06-01|06-02} \
      --phase {PHASE_NAME} \
      --status EXECUTED \
      --data PASS

For SKIPPED phases:

    PYTHONPATH=$HOME/.claude/lib/python python3 -m des.cli.log_phase \
      --project-dir docs/feature/skills/deliver \
      --step-id {step} \
      --phase {PHASE_NAME} \
      --status SKIPPED \
      --data "NOT_APPLICABLE: reason"

# RECORDING_INTEGRITY
Valid Skip Prefixes: NOT_APPLICABLE, BLOCKED_BY_DEPENDENCY, APPROVED_SKIP, CHECKPOINT_PENDING

# BOUNDARY_RULES
- Only modify: app/src/client/routes/agent-create-page.tsx, app/src/client/components/agent/wizard-step-config.tsx, app/src/client/components/agent/wizard-step-skills.tsx, app/src/client/components/agent/wizard-step-tools.tsx
- Do not modify backend code
- Do not load roadmap.json

# TIMEOUT_INSTRUCTION
Target: 30 turns max. If approaching limit, COMMIT current progress.
